title: ternatea vulgaris
intro: Ternatea vulgaris, also known as Asperula odorata or Woodruff, is a perennial herb belonging to the Rubiaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits associated with Ternatea vulgaris include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. Ternatea vulgaris can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to harness its medicinal properties.
